categories.title: Test Coverage and Enhancements for Plugin-Bootstrap

content.text: The Eliza Bootstrap Plugin has received a major enhancement in its test infrastructure through a comprehensive test suite. This update introduces full test coverage across all plugin components and behaviors, significantly improving code reliability and maintainability. The changes are low-risk as they do not affect production code but focus solely on testing improvements.

The test suite includes six main test files and one utility file, covering actions, evaluators, logic, plugin structure, providers, and services. Each file contains detailed and categorized tests, such as message handling, role updates, evaluator lifecycles, and service registries. The suite is designed to be run using Vitest with support for individual test execution, full test runs, coverage reports, and watch mode for development.

As of the latest data, Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is trading at $83,752.46, making it the highest-priced asset among the listed cryptocurrencies. Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) follows with a current price of $1,815.19. Solana (SOL) is priced at $122.76, while ai16z, a lower-value token, is trading at $0.14.

categories.title: Recent Updates and Compatibility Improvements in Eliza

content.text: A new feature has been added to include an 'id' field in the metadata source, enhancing data structure and traceability within the Eliza system.
A new CLI command, 'update-cli', has been introduced to streamline updates and improve developer workflow when working with Eliza.

A community member raised concerns about plugin compatibility with Eliza v2. Many plugins listed on the documentation site are still v1 and may not function correctly with v2. It was suggested to either remove or clearly label these plugins as v1-only to prevent developer confusion.

categories.title: Bug Fixes in ElizaOS: Telegram, Twitter, and Knowledge Manager

content.text: A fix was implemented for the Telegram 'get world' functionality to resolve an issue affecting its behavior.
The Twitter interaction logic was updated to remove an unnecessary call to 'shouldRespond', as the bootstrap process already handles this check.

A bug fix was applied to the Knowledge Manager tab to resolve a scroll issue that prevented users from viewing or loading additional knowledge documents. The update ensures proper scrollbar functionality and dynamic content loading.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Updates: Bug Fixes, Plugin Enhancements, and Test Coverage Expansion

content.text: A new feature was introduced to add an 'id' field to the metadata source, enhancing data traceability and structure within the Eliza framework.

A critical bug fix addressed a failure in creating the Twitter client. The issue stemmed from the service starting before the agent was initialized. The fix ensures the service starts only after the runtime is properly set up.
Additional Twitter-related fixes were implemented, including improvements to interaction logic by removing redundant calls to 'shouldRespond', which is already handled during bootstrap.
A bug fix was applied to the Telegram plugin's 'get world' functionality, resolving an unspecified issue.

A major enhancement was made to the plugin-bootstrap test suite, delivering comprehensive test coverage across all components. The suite includes over 4,500 lines of test code across 7 files, covering actions, evaluators, logic, plugin structure, providers, and services. This significantly improves confidence in plugin stability and behavior.
A new CLI command 'update-cli' was added, expanding the toolset available for developers working with Eliza.

A community-raised issue highlighted confusion around plugin compatibility with Eliza v2. It was clarified that only plugins in the '/packages' directory of the 'v2-develop' branch are currently v2-compatible. A recommendation was made to clearly mark or remove v1-only plugins from the documentation site.

Interactive button support was added to the Telegram plugin. This feature allows messages to include login and URL buttons, improving user interaction. The implementation includes a new Button type and conversion logic to Telegram's inline keyboard format.
Instrumentation from Eliza v1 is being integrated into v2 to enhance scenario tracking and data collection. The work is ongoing and includes addressing database design challenges.

The OpenAI plugin was refactored to improve maintainability. Key changes include centralized configuration helpers, a unified client creation function, and proper handling of file uploads in Node.js using NodeFormData.
A UI bug in the Knowledge Manager was fixed, resolving issues with scroll behavior and content loading in the knowledge tab.
Dependency updates were merged via Dependabot to keep the npm and yarn packages up to date.
